title: Pact of Madrid with the United States
description: American bases in exchange for aid ended Spain's postwar isolation and propped up the Franco regime.

title: Juan Carlos named successor
description: Franco designated the young Bourbon prince as his successor, expecting continuity; Juan Carlos would deliver democracy instead.

title: Death of Franco
description: The dictator died in his bed after months of agony; within three years Spain had a democratic constitution.

title: Democratic constitution approved
description: Spaniards overwhelmingly ratified a constitution establishing parliamentary monarchy and regional autonomy.

title: Spain joins the European Community
description: EC membership sealed Spain's return to the European mainstream after decades of dictatorship.

title: Barcelona Olympics
description: The Games remade Barcelona's waterfront and showcased a confident, democratic Spain to the world.

title: Neanderthal fossil discovered
description: Quarry workers in the Neander valley near Düsseldorf unearthed bones of an ancient human species that now bears the valley's name.

title: Bismarck becomes minister-president of Prussia
description: Appointed to break a budget deadlock, Bismarck promised to settle the great questions by 'iron and blood'.

title: Austro-Prussian War
description: Prussia's seven-week victory over Austria at Königgrätz expelled Vienna from German affairs and dissolved the Confederation.

title: German Empire proclaimed at Versailles
description: After victory over France, the German princes proclaimed Wilhelm I emperor in the Hall of Mirrors.

title: Bismarck's health insurance law
description: The world's first national health insurance system was built to undercut socialism — and became a model for the welfare state.

title: Röntgen discovers X-rays
description: Wilhelm Röntgen's mysterious rays, discovered at Würzburg, won the first Nobel Prize in Physics.

title: First Zeppelin flight
description: Count Zeppelin's rigid airship LZ 1 rose over Lake Constance, beginning Germany's love affair with the airship.

title: Germany enters the First World War
description: The Schlieffen Plan sent German armies through Belgium toward Paris — and into four years of two-front war.

title: Kaiser abdicates; republic declared
description: Defeat and revolution ended the Hohenzollern monarchy; Wilhelm II fled to the Netherlands.

title: Hyperinflation
description: The mark collapsed to trillions per dollar; wheelbarrows of banknotes became the enduring image of Weimar's crisis year.

title: Hitler appointed chancellor
description: President Hindenburg appointed Hitler on 30 January; within months the Nazis had destroyed German democracy.

title: Kristallnacht
description: Nazi mobs burned synagogues and smashed Jewish shops across Germany in a state-orchestrated pogrom.

title: Surrender and division
description: Germany surrendered unconditionally in May 1945 and was divided into four occupation zones by the victorious Allies.

title: Berlin Airlift
description: When the Soviets blockaded West Berlin, Allied aircraft supplied two million people for eleven months.

title: West Germany joins NATO
description: Ten years after the war, the Federal Republic rearmed within the Western alliance; the Warsaw Pact formed in response.

title: Berlin Wall built
description: East Germany sealed off West Berlin overnight to stop the flood of emigration, dividing the city for twenty-eight years.

title: Munich Olympics
description: West Germany's 'cheerful Games' were shattered by the terrorist murder of eleven Israeli athletes.

title: Fall of the Berlin Wall
description: A bungled press conference opened the border on 9 November; Berliners danced on the Wall as the Cold War order dissolved.

title: German reunification
description: East Germany acceded to the Federal Republic on 3 October, restoring a single German state after forty-five years.

title: Crimean War begins
description: Russia's war with the Ottomans drew in Britain and France; defeat at home exposed the empire's backwardness.

title: Emancipation of the serfs
description: Alexander II freed more than twenty million serfs, the greatest single act of emancipation in European history.

title: Tolstoy completes War and Peace
description: Tolstoy's epic of the Napoleonic invasion appeared in full, a summit of the Russian novel.

title: Alexander II assassinated
description: A People's Will bomb killed the tsar-liberator on a St Petersburg street; repression followed reform.

title: Trans-Siberian Railway begun
description: Russia began the world's longest railway, nearly 6,000 miles from Moscow to Vladivostok.

title: Revolution of 1905
description: Bloody Sunday, naval mutiny, and a general strike forced Nicholas II to concede Russia's first parliament.

title: October Revolution
description: Lenin's Bolsheviks seized Petrograd's key points and the Winter Palace, taking Russia out of the war and into revolution.

title: Death of Lenin
description: Lenin died at fifty-three; his embalmed body went on display as Stalin outmaneuvered rivals for succession.

title: Famine amid collectivization
description: Forced collectivization of agriculture brought catastrophic famine to Ukraine, Kazakhstan, and southern Russia.

title: Germany invades the Soviet Union
description: Operation Barbarossa opened the largest land war in history; the Soviet Union would lose some 27 million people.

title: Fall of Berlin
description: The Red Army took the German capital at a cost of 80,000 dead and raised the Soviet flag over the Reichstag.

title: Sputnik launched
description: The first artificial satellite circled the Earth every 96 minutes, stunning the West and opening the space age.

title: Gagarin orbits the Earth
description: Yuri Gagarin became the first human in space, completing one orbit in 108 minutes aboard Vostok 1.

title: Invasion of Afghanistan
description: Soviet troops entered Afghanistan to prop up a communist government, beginning a nine-year war often called the USSR's Vietnam.

title: Chernobyl disaster
description: Reactor No. 4 exploded in the world's worst nuclear accident; the cover-up corroded faith in the Soviet system.

title: Dissolution of the Soviet Union
description: After a failed coup and the secession of its republics, the USSR ceased to exist on 26 December 1991.

title: Crimean War
description: With British and French help, the empire fought Russia to a standstill — and was admitted, briefly, to the concert of Europe.

title: Suez Canal opens
description: The canal through nominally Ottoman Egypt joined the Mediterranean to the Red Sea and rewired world trade.

title: First Ottoman constitution
description: Sultan Abdulhamid II granted a constitution and parliament — then suspended both within two years.

title: Birth of Mustafa Kemal
description: The future Atatürk was born in Ottoman Salonika; he would dismantle the empire that raised him and build a republic.

title: Greco-Turkish War
description: The empire won a quick war with Greece over Crete — its last clear military victory in Europe.

title: Young Turk Revolution
description: Army officers forced the sultan to restore the constitution, bringing the Committee of Union and Progress to power.

title: Gallipoli campaign
description: Ottoman forces repelled the Allied landings at Gallipoli; the defense made Mustafa Kemal's name.

title: Republic of Turkey proclaimed
description: After victory in the War of Independence, Mustafa Kemal proclaimed a republic with its capital at Ankara.

title: Alphabet reform
description: Turkey replaced Arabic script with a Latin alphabet in months, part of Atatürk's sweeping secular reforms.

title: Death of Atatürk
description: The founder of modern Turkey died at Dolmabahçe Palace; clocks there are still stopped at 9:05.

title: Turkey joins the United Nations
description: Having stayed neutral for most of the war, Turkey declared war on Germany in February 1945 and became a UN founding member.

title: First free elections
description: The Democrat Party's landslide ended a quarter-century of one-party rule, Turkey's first peaceful transfer of power.

title: Turkey joins NATO
description: Turkey entered the Atlantic alliance alongside Greece, anchoring NATO's southeastern flank.

title: Military coup
description: The army overthrew the Menderes government; the prime minister was executed the following year.

title: Cyprus intervention
description: Turkey landed troops on Cyprus after a Greek-backed coup, dividing the island along a line that still stands.

title: Military coup of 1980
description: General Evren's coup ended years of political violence at the cost of mass arrests and a new authoritarian constitution.

title: İzmit earthquake
description: A magnitude 7.6 earthquake near İzmit killed more than 17,000 people and reshaped Turkish politics and building codes.

title: Prime Minister Canalejas assassinated
description: An anarchist shot the reforming liberal prime minister José Canalejas outside a Madrid bookshop.

title: Russo-Turkish War
description: Russia's victory in the Balkans freed Bulgaria from Ottoman rule and brought its armies within sight of Istanbul.

title: Methuen Treaty with England
description: The treaty traded Portuguese wine for English cloth, binding Portugal's economy and diplomacy to England for a century.

title: Portuguese fleet victorious at Matapan
description: John V's navy helped Venice and the Pope defeat the Ottomans off Cape Matapan, a showpiece of Portugal's gold-funded prestige.

title: Mafra Palace consecrated
description: The colossal palace-convent of Mafra, paid for with Brazilian gold, embodied John V's ambition to rival Rome and Versailles.

title: Águas Livres Aqueduct completed
description: Lisbon's great aqueduct began carrying water across the Alcântara valley; its arches survived the earthquake seven years later.

title: Great Lisbon earthquake
description: Earthquake, tsunami, and fire destroyed Lisbon on All Saints' Day, killing tens of thousands and shaking Europe's faith in providence.

title: Pombal expels the Jesuits
description: The Marquis of Pombal expelled the Society of Jesus from Portugal and its empire, the first of Europe's crowns to do so.

title: Slavery abolished in mainland Portugal
description: Pombal banned the import of slaves to metropolitan Portugal — while the trade continued to Brazil.

title: Maria I crowned; Pombal falls
description: Portugal's first queen regnant dismissed the all-powerful Pombal and reversed much of his authoritarian reform program.

title: Prince John assumes the regency
description: With Maria I declared incurably mad, her son John governed in her name through the storms of the revolutionary era.

title: Royal court flees to Brazil
description: Ahead of Junot's invading army, the entire Portuguese court sailed for Rio de Janeiro under British escort — a capital moved across an ocean.

title: French driven from Portugal
description: Wellington's lines of Torres Vedras starved Masséna's army out of Portugal, ending the third and last French invasion.

title: Brazil declares independence
description: Prince Pedro's 'Independence or Death!' cry on the Ipiranga cost Portugal its vast American colony and half its revenue.

title: Miguelite War begins
description: Miguel I seized absolute power from his niece Maria II, plunging Portugal into six years of civil war between absolutists and liberals.

title: Liberals win; Miguel exiled
description: The Concession of Evoramonte ended the civil war, restored Maria II, and banished Miguel from Portugal for life.

title: Maria da Fonte uprising
description: A popular revolt that began among the women of the Minho spiraled into a brief civil war, settled only by British and Spanish intervention.

title: William III dies; stadtholderless again
description: The king-stadtholder's death without heirs opened the Second Stadtholderless Period, with the Republic run by the regent oligarchy.

title: Peace of Utrecht signed at home
description: The treaties ending the War of the Spanish Succession were signed in Utrecht — a diplomatic triumph hosted by an exhausted Republic.

title: Bubble year touches Amsterdam
description: Speculative manias in Paris and London spilled into Dutch markets; Amsterdam's exchange weathered the crash better than most.

title: Shipworm plague threatens the dikes
description: The teredo worm was found devouring the wooden dike revetments, forcing a ruinous rebuilding of the sea defenses in stone.

title: Orangist revolution restores the stadtholderate
description: French invasion panic swept William IV to power as hereditary stadtholder of all seven provinces — the first ever.

title: Infant William V inherits the stadtholderate
description: William IV's death left a three-year-old heir; decades of regency deepened the Republic's political drift.

title: Amsterdam banking crisis
description: The collapse of the De Neufville house after the Seven Years' War rippled through Europe — an early international financial contagion.

title: Eisinga begins his planetarium
description: Frisian wool-comber Eise Eisinga started building a working orrery into his living-room ceiling; it remains the world's oldest functioning planetarium.

title: Fourth Anglo-Dutch War
description: Britain declared war over Dutch trade with the American rebels; defeat shattered the Republic's commerce and prestige.

title: Prussian army crushes the Patriots
description: A Prussian invasion restored William V against the democratic Patriot movement; thousands of Patriots fled to France.

title: Batavian Revolution
description: French armies crossed the frozen rivers and the old Republic dissolved into the Batavian Republic; William V fled to England.

title: Louis Bonaparte made King of Holland
description: Napoleon installed his brother as king; Louis took his new country's side often enough to be removed within four years.

title: Kingdom of the Netherlands proclaimed
description: The Congress of Vienna united the northern and southern Netherlands under William I as a buffer against France.

title: Belgium secedes
description: Revolution in Brussels split the kingdom in two; William I refused to accept the loss for nine expensive years.

title: Thorbecke's liberal constitution
description: Frightened by revolutions abroad, William II accepted a constitution that made ministers answerable to parliament, founding Dutch parliamentary democracy.

title: Disaster at Poltava
description: Charles XII's army was destroyed deep in Ukraine; the king fled to Ottoman territory and Sweden's Baltic empire began to unravel.

title: Charles XII shot at Fredriksten
description: The warrior king was killed by a projectile in the trenches before a Norwegian fortress, ending two decades of ceaseless campaigning.

title: Peace of Nystad ends the empire
description: Sweden ceded its eastern Baltic provinces to Russia, closing the Great Northern War and its own great-power age.

title: The Hats take power
description: In the parliamentary Age of Liberty, the war-minded Hat party ousted the cautious Caps — party politics a half-century before the French Revolution.

title: Celsius proposes his temperature scale
description: Uppsala astronomer Anders Celsius defined a hundred-degree scale between water's boiling and freezing points.

title: Linnaeus publishes Species Plantarum
description: Carl Linnaeus catalogued every plant known to him under two-word Latin names, founding modern biological nomenclature.

title: World's first freedom of the press act
description: Sweden's Riksdag abolished censorship and opened official documents to the public — the world's first such law.

title: Gustav III's bloodless coup
description: The young king surrounded the Riksdag with loyal troops and ended the Age of Liberty, restoring royal power by charm and bayonet.

title: Swedish Academy founded
description: Gustav III founded the Academy of eighteen chairs 'for the purity, strength and sublimity of the Swedish language'; it would later award the Nobel Prize in Literature.

title: Gustav III assassinated at the opera
description: A masked conspirator shot the king at a midnight masquerade in his own opera house; he died two weeks later.

title: Finland lost to Russia
description: Defeat in the Finnish War cost Sweden a third of its territory and triggered a coup, a new constitution, and a new dynasty.

title: Union with Norway
description: The Treaty of Kiel and a short campaign brought Norway into personal union under the Swedish crown — Sweden's last war.

title: Berzelius isolates silicon
description: Jöns Jacob Berzelius, the great systematizer of chemistry, first isolated the element that would one day name an age.

title: Göta Canal completed
description: After 22 years of digging, the canal linked the Baltic to the North Sea across Sweden's heart — the largest civil engineering work in its history.

title: Compulsory schooling introduced
description: Every parish was ordered to open an elementary school, an early foundation of the country's later social model.

title: Augustus II restored after Poltava
description: Russia's victory over Sweden returned Augustus the Strong to the Polish throne — and made the Commonwealth a Russian protectorate in all but name.

title: The Silent Sejm
description: Under Russian guns, a one-day parliament passed sweeping limits on the army and the king without a single deputy being allowed to speak.

title: Tumult of Thorn
description: A religious riot in Toruń ended with Protestant leaders executed, a scandal that Europe's powers used to paint the Commonwealth as intolerant.

title: War of the Polish Succession
description: Rival elections of Stanisław Leszczyński and Augustus III dragged Europe into war; Russian arms decided who would be king of Poland.

title: Załuski Library opens
description: The brothers Załuski opened one of Europe's first great public libraries in Warsaw; Russia would cart it off after the partitions.

title: Foreign armies cross a neutral Commonwealth
description: During the Seven Years' War, Russian and Prussian armies marched across Poland at will — neutrality without the power to enforce it.

title: Stanisław II Augustus elected
description: Catherine the Great's former favorite was elected king with Russian backing; he would be the Commonwealth's last monarch and its greatest patron of the arts.

title: First Partition
description: Russia, Prussia, and Austria each carved off a slice of the Commonwealth, taking a third of its land and people at the stroke of a pen.

title: Constitution of 3 May
description: Europe's first modern written constitution abolished the liberum veto and created a constitutional monarchy — too late to save the state that wrote it.

title: Kościuszko Uprising
description: The hero of two continents led a national rising with scythe-armed peasants in its ranks; its defeat sealed the Commonwealth's fate.

title: Third Partition erases the Commonwealth
description: The three partitioning powers divided what remained; after eight centuries, Poland vanished from the map of Europe.

title: Chopin born at Żelazowa Wola
description: Frédéric Chopin was born in a manor house west of Warsaw; his music would carry Poland's voice through a century without a Polish state.

title: November Uprising
description: Cadets in Warsaw rose against Russian rule; a year of full-scale war ended in defeat, repression, and the Great Emigration.

title: Kraków Uprising
description: A rising in the free city of Kraków was crushed within days; Austria annexed the last unpartitioned scrap of old Poland.

title: Royal Academy of History founded
description: John V founded the Academia Real da História to document Portugal's past, part of his campaign of gold-funded cultural prestige.

title: Estrela Basilica consecrated
description: Maria I's great domed basilica, built in thanks for the birth of an heir, rose over Lisbon as the earthquake city rebuilt itself.

title: Anglo-Dutch Treaty divides the East
description: Britain and the Netherlands drew a line through the Malay world, trading Singapore's hinterland for Sumatra and shaping two future nations.

title: The Great Sejm convenes
description: The four-year reform parliament set out to rescue the Commonwealth, doubling the army and drafting the constitution of 1791.

title: Napoleon creates the Duchy of Warsaw
description: From Prussia's partition spoils Napoleon carved a small Polish state — hope enough to send Polish legions across Europe under his eagles.

title: Mickiewicz launches Polish Romanticism
description: Adam Mickiewicz's Ballads and Romances opened the Romantic age in Polish letters — a literature that kept the nation alive without a state.
